core_mechanism: linear stability analysis via the complex eigenfrequency spectrum of an open dissipative system, where each perturbation mode has a complex characteristic frequency and the sign of its imaginary part (damping vs. growth rate) determines whether disturbances decay or amplify
brief_reason: The abstract computes the real and imaginary parts of the quasinormal frequencies of a linearized perturbation as a function of a control parameter and infers stability from the imaginary part never changing sign, i.e. the damping rate staying decaying.
